top_findings.summary: This Google Calendar scheduling skill requests access to read from the clipboard and process screenshots from the Desktop ("latest screenshot on Desktop"), which are excessive permissions for its primary purpose of managing calendar events. While text input for event creation is reasonable, automatic clipboard reading (without explicit user paste action) and screenshot processing introduce unnecessary data collection risks. The skill should instead rely on explicit user input or file paths provided by the user rather than automatically accessing system resources like clipboard contents or desktop screenshots.
